title: Protecting Personally Identifiable Information (PII)
description: Learn to recognize and avoid sharing PII when using AI tools.

scenarios.title: Customer Name in Prompt
scenarios.description: You need AI help drafting an email to a client. Which prompt is safer?
scenarios.unsafe_example: Draft an email to John Smith at Walmart about our Q3 pricing update.
scenarios.safe_example: Draft an email to a retail client about a quarterly pricing update.
scenarios.explanation: Remove specific names and company identifiers. Use generic descriptions instead.

scenarios.title: Employee Contact Information
scenarios.description: You want AI to format a contact list. Which approach protects privacy?
scenarios.unsafe_example: Format this list: Sarah Johnson, 555-0123, sarah.j@coca-cola.com, SSN 123-45-6789
scenarios.safe_example: Help me create a template for formatting employee contact lists with fields: name, phone, email.
scenarios.explanation: Never paste real employee data. Ask for templates or use placeholder data instead.

quiz.question: Which of the following is considered PII?
quiz.explanation: Social Security Numbers are personally identifiable information that must never be shared with AI tools.

quiz.question: What should you do before pasting customer data into an AI prompt?
quiz.explanation: Always anonymize customer data before using AI tools. Replace names with generic labels.
